title: The Ten Per Cent Solution

jokes.text: Ay-yi-yi!
jokes.setup: Opening exclamation
jokes.punchline: Bart's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart catchphrase - high character integration but very familiar

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Something has gone wrong for Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: The most iconic Simpsons catchphrase - perfect character fit, maximum quotability

jokes.text: Can't get enough Itchy and Scratchy! And now it's time for... another Itchy and Scratchy? Who's the star of this show, me or...?!
jokes.setup: Krusty realizes his show is being dominated by the cartoon segment
jokes.punchline: His frustrated realization that he's been sidelined on his own show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on how supporting elements can overshadow the main attraction

jokes.text: Why is my show all cartoons?! And all the movies they're making fun of are over a year old. It's like those parodies were written when the movies came out, but it took so long to animate them that we look dated and hacky. Why can't we...?!
jokes.setup: Krusty's continued frustration with his show
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on The Simpsons' own production timeline making their parodies feel dated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show's own struggles with animation lead times making cultural references stale

jokes.text: Kids, that's enough TV. Maggie's eye is starting to wander.
jokes.setup: Marge notices the kids have been watching too much TV
jokes.punchline: The visual of baby Maggie developing a lazy eye from excessive screen time
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about TV's effects on children with efficient visual punchline

jokes.text: We're gonna get some fresh air and visit the Museum... of Television.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to get away from TV and get fresh air
jokes.punchline: But they're going to a museum dedicated to television
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic setup-punchline about escaping TV by going to celebrate TV

jokes.text: Why can't we visit a real museum? One that doesn't have the skeletons of the Three Stooges in the lobby.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to visit a real museum
jokes.punchline: The macabre image of the Three Stooges' actual skeletons being displayed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good character voice - Lisa's intellectual snobbery with a morbid twist

jokes.text: But where will people find clips of old TV shows if they're not housed in a giant building? Where? Where?!
jokes.setup: Learning the TV museum is closing
jokes.punchline: Homer's obliviousness to the internet and digital media
jokes.explanation: Good character-based absurdist humor about Homer's technological obliviousness

jokes.text: Ay, caramba!
jokes.setup: Bart sees something surprising
jokes.punchline: Another Bart catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart catchphrase usage - familiar but appropriate

jokes.text: The gun Sheriff Baby wore!
jokes.setup: Looking at TV memorabilia
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of a baby sheriff character wearing a gun
jokes.explanation: Absurd TV show concept typical of Comic Book Guy's obsessive knowledge

jokes.text: Oh, my pants are splitting wide open. And people will see my tattoo of Donald Duck smoking a doob!
jokes.setup: Homer's pants are splitting
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous and inappropriate tattoo that would be revealed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane embarrassment to absurd personal revelation

jokes.text: That was for Marge's eyes only.
jokes.setup: Following the tattoo revelation
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced sense of intimacy about the ridiculous tattoo
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that adds to the absurdity with Homer's earnestness

jokes.text: Only the funniest black-and-white man ever.
jokes.setup: Describing Fatso Flanagan
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and dated categorization
jokes.explanation: Captures how people categorize old entertainers in weirdly specific ways

jokes.text: Everything's a rip-off of The Honeymooners.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling something a Honeymooners rip-off
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly broad generalization about all comedy
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about comedy tropes with meta-commentary on originality

jokes.text: Margie, you're the greatest. Oh, Ralph, Fred, Archie, King of Queens, I mean Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer imitating The Honeymooners
jokes.punchline: Listing other sitcom husbands before remembering his own name
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that proves the point about sitcom archetypes while showing Homer's self-awareness

jokes.text: Are you getting excited? No. That's me not holding my breath.
jokes.setup: Fatso asking if his wife is excited about his plans
jokes.punchline: Her deadpan, withering response about not holding her breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of spousal skepticism - classic sitcom wife archetype

jokes.text: They were so childless and miserable.
jokes.setup: Watching the old comedy show
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of what made the old show 'funny'
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate observation about the source of old sitcom comedy

jokes.text: If you ever hear a star's name and wonder, is he dead? The answer is either I represent him or yes.
jokes.setup: Annie describing her client roster
jokes.punchline: She only represents dead or dying careers
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect structure - defines character and situation brilliantly

jokes.text: Um, would you play a bloated corpse on CSI?
jokes.setup: Annie asking if there's a place for someone like her on TV
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutally honest suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - brutally honest without malice, just practical

jokes.text: Would I?!
jokes.setup: Bart's suggestion about playing a corpse
jokes.punchline: Annie's enthusiastic acceptance of the role
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing her desperation for any work

jokes.text: Wikipedia, Twitter-- who names these things? Percy Dovetonsils?
jokes.setup: Krusty complaining about modern technology names
jokes.punchline: Reference to an obscure 1950s comedy character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty - shows his age and cultural disconnect with obscure reference

jokes.text: Oh, that was easy.
jokes.setup: After saying firing someone is never easy
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction showing it actually was easy
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor showing corporate callousness

jokes.text: Yep, everything's nice and legal!
jokes.setup: Being fired in a humiliating way
jokes.punchline: Krusty's defeated acceptance of his contractual firing method
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's resignation to his professional humiliation

jokes.text: so I don't have a funny third item.
jokes.setup: Listing things they took from him
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that comedy comes in threes and he can't deliver
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about comedy structure while showing Krusty's professional helplessness

jokes.text: Krustila, I'm gonna drop you as a friend first, so that when I drop you as a client, you'll know it's only business.
jokes.setup: Agent trying to soften the blow of firing Krusty
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic that makes it worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Great setup-punchline showing Hollywood's twisted logic for cruelty

jokes.text: And when I finished, every one of them was spic and span.
jokes.setup: Talking about sharing boats, girlfriends, and condos
jokes.punchline: The disturbing implication about his thoroughness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the agent's treatment of shared possessions/relationships

jokes.text: It's a smartphone, you out-of-it hack.
jokes.setup: Krusty confused about technology
jokes.punchline: Agent's cruel dismissal highlighting the generational gap
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly the industry discards older talent for being out of touch

jokes.text: Little help? This only shuts the door.
jokes.setup: Krusty struggling with the garage door opener
jokes.punchline: His continued technological incompetence
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Krusty's helplessness with modern technology

jokes.text: I liked the knowledgeable docents.
jokes.setup: Asking about favorite museum moments
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual appreciation for educational aspects others ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response - finding educational value where others see entertainment

jokes.text: I liked the early closing time.
jokes.setup: Following Lisa's earnest response
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest admission that he wanted to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - honest about his boredom in contrast to Lisa's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Now I'm strung out in a ball pit!
jokes.setup: Krusty's fall from grace
jokes.punchline: The pathetic image of him literally in a children's ball pit
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor for how low Krusty has fallen

jokes.text: Well, you're not exactly my hero. I see you more as a cautionary tale.
jokes.setup: Krusty saying kids shouldn't see their hero so low
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutally honest assessment of what Krusty represents
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart line - honest, cutting, but not cruel. Perfect structure and delivery.

jokes.text: Well, people also love a quitter-- Sarah Palin, The Beatles...
jokes.setup: After encouraging comeback examples
jokes.punchline: Counter-examples of successful quitting with odd pairings
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of political figure with beloved band in context of 'quitting'

jokes.text: Also better get back on those anti-psychotic meds.
jokes.setup: Krusty preparing for meetings
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of serious mental health medication
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental health treated casually, typical of Krusty's problems

jokes.text: It's the laugh of the most ungrateful, credit-grabbing, other-people's-room-service-eating man that I ever met.
jokes.setup: Recognizing Krusty's laugh
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and escalating list of grievances ending with petty room service theft
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from general character flaws to very specific petty behavior

jokes.text: Oh, now I hate him, too.
jokes.setup: Hearing about Annie's hatred for Krusty
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate bandwagon jumping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - easily swayed by someone else's strong opinion

jokes.text: These geniuses can blow up the planet, but they don't know the alphabet.
jokes.setup: Commenting on nuclear weapons progression
jokes.punchline: The illogical skipping from A-bomb to H-bomb
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor showing young Krusty's intelligence and wit

jokes.text: There wasn't a dry turtleneck in the house.
jokes.setup: Describing Krusty's successful performance
jokes.punchline: Play on 'dry eye' but referencing the beatnik audience's clothing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures the 1960s beatnik scene

jokes.text: I don't want to play in Peoria. They're always doing construction on Highway 74.
jokes.setup: Annie using 'play in Peoria' metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Krusty taking it literally and complaining about traffic
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Krusty missing the metaphor for literal concerns

jokes.text: This is the best kind of comedy of all: cheap!
jokes.setup: Trying physical comedy
jokes.punchline: Krusty's enthusiasm for low-effort humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy quality with Krusty's characteristic laziness

jokes.text: There's only one thing I want: ice for my ding-dong!
jokes.setup: Annie listing what he'll get from success
jokes.punchline: Krusty's innocent but suggestive request
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo humor, somewhat cheap but fits character

jokes.text: Today's kids are less sensitive than an army condom.
jokes.setup: Defending against criticism about adult content
jokes.punchline: Crude but specific military metaphor about desensitization
jokes.explanation: Bold observational humor about generational differences in sensitivity

jokes.text: I can only tell the truth right after sex.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he waited to reveal his betrayal
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific personal limitation
jokes.explanation: Character-specific quirk that explains bad behavior

jokes.text: Deep down, I'm doing this to get back at my father.
jokes.setup: Being accused of being ungrateful
jokes.punchline: Pop psychology explanation for betrayal
jokes.explanation: Satirizes easy psychological explanations for bad behavior

jokes.text: I didn't have sex with a clown for five months. What about mimes? Come on. I'm not made of stone.
jokes.setup: Describing her anger at Krusty
jokes.punchline: The specific limitation of her boycott and immediate exception
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and specificity - the mime exception is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Trust with me is like a candle. When you blow it out, it's gone for good.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she won't take Krusty back
jokes.punchline: The candle metaphor for trust
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor that sets up the next joke

jokes.text: What about a comedy candle? You know, that relights itself?
jokes.setup: Annie's candle metaphor
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal-minded solution using novelty candles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer callback - takes metaphor literally with silly but logical solution

jokes.text: Once, I used one on Milhouse. He was wishing for his parents to get back together, but the flame never went out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of comedy candles
jokes.punchline: Dark story of psychological torture using the novelty candle
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Bart story that escalates the candle concept to cruel prank

jokes.text: I promise you Krusty will show up on time or sober. Yeah, that's right, I said 'or.'
jokes.setup: Making promises about Krusty's professionalism
jokes.punchline: The low bar of either punctuality OR sobriety, not both
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure with excellent callback to emphasize the low standards

jokes.text: They're gonna want F-bombs and all I can give 'em is 'ca-ca' and 'ta-tas.'
jokes.setup: Worried about adult audience expectations
jokes.punchline: The contrast between adult content and his childish vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's limitations transitioning to adult content

jokes.text: What did he get in trouble for? That's all?! I did that while you were on the phone!
jokes.setup: Learning about Pee-Wee Herman's scandal
jokes.punchline: Krusty's casual admission to doing worse things recently
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Krusty's complete lack of shame or boundaries

jokes.text: And if they didn't, until the '70s, I hit 'em with a stick.
jokes.setup: Describing how he got kids to laugh with candy
jokes.punchline: The casual admission of child abuse as motivation technique
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about changing standards in children's entertainment

jokes.text: It's called Circus of Shame or something.
jokes.setup: Mentioning a documentary about his past
jokes.punchline: The vague, dismissive way he references his own scandal
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's casual attitude toward his various scandals

jokes.text: Everything's perfect about the past except how it led to the present.
jokes.setup: Talking about nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about the irony of nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation with perfect economy and structure - highly quotable

jokes.text: Would that I could turn back time and never play this career-killing role.
jokes.setup: A clock character appearing on stage
jokes.punchline: The character's regret about the very role he's currently performing
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about actors' regret while they're still performing the role

jokes.text: I've pulled out everything but my Little Krusty.
jokes.setup: Playing 'What's In Krusty's Pocket' game
jokes.punchline: Suggestive reference to anatomy
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo humor but fits the character's lowbrow tendencies

jokes.text: Everything I loved as a kid and hated as a teenager I love as an adult!
jokes.setup: Man excited about the nostalgic show
jokes.punchline: Describing the cycle of nostalgia through life stages
jokes.explanation: Good observation about nostalgia cycles, relatable

jokes.text: Everything I loved as a kid and hated as a teenager I love as an adult!
jokes.setup: Enjoying the nostalgic show
jokes.punchline: The cycle of appreciation through life stages
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about how taste evolves through life - very relatable

jokes.text: And this time I'm not blowing it on cocaine for my horses.
jokes.setup: Talking about not wasting his comeback success
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and expensive past mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - cocaine for horses is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: They start well, then they get paranoid around the third turn.
jokes.setup: Following the cocaine for horses line
jokes.punchline: Detailed explanation of how cocaine affects horse racing performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that makes the absurd premise even funnier with specific details

jokes.text: Which ones? Telebozo? Shtick at Nite? E.S.P.U.?
jokes.setup: Asking which networks are interested
jokes.punchline: Parody names of real cable networks
jokes.explanation: Decent parody network names, particularly 'Shtick at Nite' for Nick at Nite

jokes.text: And we pay for everything with soft porno and boxing.
jokes.setup: Describing their premium cable network
jokes.punchline: The honest admission about how cable networks fund programming
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about cable TV business model

jokes.text: If not, we'll burn it to the ground! And that will be the end of that phoenix.
jokes.setup: Talking about having critics in their pocket, even the Boston Phoenix
jokes.punchline: The literal threat followed by the phoenix pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with phoenix mythology while showing executive ruthlessness

jokes.text: I got your coffees, Mr. K.! Too hot! Too cold!
jokes.setup: Describing how successful Krusty has become
jokes.punchline: Famous actors reduced to being coffee-fetching interns
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Hollywood power dynamics and actor careers

jokes.text: Beloved entertainer... sad old man! Beloved entertainer... sad old man!
jokes.setup: Krusty's green hair against green screen
jokes.punchline: The visual effect making him appear and disappear
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about green screen technology problems

jokes.text: For years I lived on piñata candy!
jokes.setup: Defending birthday parties as legitimate work
jokes.punchline: The desperate image of subsisting on children's party candy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Krusty's past desperation with specific detail

jokes.text: Your going rate is 100 barrels of oil.
jokes.setup: Describing Krusty's new high-end client base
jokes.punchline: Payment in oil barrels instead of money
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the extreme wealth of Middle Eastern clients

jokes.text: And don't you ever upstage him or craft service will be serving chimp tacos!
jokes.setup: Warning the monkey not to upstage Krusty
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd threat about what will happen to the monkey
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist threat that's both specific and ridiculous

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd like to squeeze a lime on that!
jokes.setup: Hearing about chimp tacos
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic culinary interest in the disturbing threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - missing the moral issue and focusing on food

jokes.text: Ooh! Idea for sketch: rapping mummy. Notorious T-U-T. It writes itself. Find someone to write it.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone wrapped up like a mummy
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun and immediate contradiction about it writing itself
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Krusty's lazy creative process and bad puns

jokes.text: if a woman does it, she's a bitch. But in a man, those traits would be considered...
jokes.setup: Defending Annie's behavior as gender bias
jokes.punchline: The inability to complete the thought because those traits are bad in anyone
jokes.explanation: Smart subversion of the gender double-standard argument

jokes.text: I'm only getting college credit for this!
jokes.setup: Being attacked by Annie
jokes.punchline: The pathetic cry about unpaid internship exploitation
jokes.explanation: Good observation about unpaid internship abuse in entertainment industry

jokes.text: Hey-how-are-ya-don't-talk-to-me-the-show's-starting.
jokes.setup: Krusty doing pre-show audience interaction
jokes.punchline: Rushed greeting that immediately cuts off interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's phony audience interaction

jokes.text: And I'll keep my promise, like Obama did with Guantanamo.
jokes.setup: Promising to be angry and polemical
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaking into political comedy with Obama reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janeane Garofalo character - immediately going political as promised

jokes.text: Burn on you! I brought my expandable beret!
jokes.setup: Being told she can only fill her beret once with shrimp
jokes.punchline: Her prepared countermeasure with expandable beret
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing her preparedness and defiance

jokes.text: I'm paying extra for this channel? Hasn't been good since The Wire. Ah, who am I kidding? I never watched The Wire. Had to bluff my way through so many conversations.
jokes.setup: Complaining about cable quality
jokes.punchline: The admission about pretending to have watched prestigious TV
jokes.explanation: Excellent relatable humor about cultural pressure to watch prestige TV

jokes.text: Sex over 60: The Mechanics of the Impossible. Take two!
jokes.setup: Krusty's new show concept
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable and overly clinical title
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor fitting for Krusty's descent into increasingly desperate content

tmdb_overview: After Krusty the Clown's show is canceled and his talent agency drops him, his very first agent, Annie, tries to craft his comeback.
